title: Lathe Chuck & Jaw Grease 30 g | Heavy-Duty Grease for Chucks, Jaws, Scrolls & Key Squares – Coolant-Resistant, Anti-Corrosion, Long-Life Lubrication
features: RELIABLE CLAMPING – Helps jaws bite evenly and hold true by reducing stick–slip on chucks, scrolls and key squares.
COOLANT & WATER RESISTANT – Stays in place under cutting fluids and wash-downs for longer service intervals.
HIGH LOAD PROTECTION – Built for slow, high-pressure sliding to reduce wear, galling and fretting on contact faces.
TACKY, STAY-PUT FILM – Strong adhesion resists fling-off, keeping scrolls and jaw guides lubricated throughout the shift.
RUST & CORROSION GUARD – Forms a protective barrier to help prevent staining and seizure between cleans.
description: Keep your workholding smooth, consistent and dependable. Atkinson Oil Co. Lathe Chuck & Jaw Grease is a workshop-ready lubricant made for the realities of turning: intermittent motion, high clamping loads and constant exposure to coolant. It lays down a tenacious, protective film across jaws, scrolls and key squares to minimise stick–slip, reduce wear and maintain rotary accuracy between services. A practical 30 g pack is ideal for routine maintenance, travelling service kits or trial adoption on individual machines. The grease resists wash-off, clings to sliding ways and helps protect against rust, so chucks run cleaner and last longer with fewer strip-downs. How to Use Power down and lock out the machine. Clean contact areas to remove old grease, swarf and coolant residue. Apply a thin, even layer to jaw guides, scroll teeth and key squares. Cycle jaws open/closed to distribute; wipe excess. Re-apply periodically based on usage, environment and cleaning schedule.

specifications_table.Recommended Uses For Product: General workholding on manual and CNC lathes, Jaw guides, scrolls and key squares, Lathe chucks (3-jaw, 4-jaw and scroll types), Maintenance kits for shop-floor and mobile service
specifications_table.Specific Uses For Product: Protection against coolant, water and corrosion during routine operation, Smoother clamping and release with reduced stick–slip, Wear reduction on high-pressure sliding surfaces
